articles.title: Camper and ISSEY MIYAKE Elevate the Slip-On Mule In Their First Footwear Collab
articles.summary: Name: Camper x ISSEY MIYAKE Peu FormMSRP: TBCRelease Date: July 15Where to Buy: Camper, ISSEY MIYAKECamper and ISSEY MIYAKE have joined forces to debut Peu Form, their inaugural footwear collaboration. Designed by ISSEY MIYAKE's Satoshi Kondo for FW26, the collab draws inspiration from the Japanese house's foundational "a piece of cloth" concept, reimagining Camper’s iconic Peu silhouette by exploring how a single piece of material can organically wrap and drape around the human foot.The resulting shoe boasts an appearance that is both organic and fluid, balancing originality with functionality. Elevating the design, the quarters are embellished with tonal stitching that injects a touch of whimsy into the design. "Haz

articles.title: 7 Times Nike Referenced a Horror Franchise on a Sneaker
articles.summary: It’s hard to believe Nike has been making horror-movie inspired sneakers for almost 20 years. Throughout the two decades, they’ve mined the iconography of slasher and monster films to sneak elements of their visuals into color schemes, materials and tiny details without explicitly working with any specific studios for official collaborations. No franchise logos on the tongue. No studio credits on the box. Only the skill of sneaker designers.It began in 2007, with a three-shoe pack of two Dunks and an Air Trainer 1 that almost halted the entire process before it got off the ground. New Line Cinema had issued cease-and-desist orders against a Freddy Krueger-themed release, transforming a potential fun holiday shoe release into one of the most legendary moments in sneaker lore. But Nike persi

Developed in collaboration with the Los Angeles Dodgers two-way star and New Balance global ambassador, the series covers game-day performance, turf training, gym agility, and post-game recovery across four distinct silhouettes, each running on the brand's FuelCell cushioning platform (with one exception) and dressed in a shared white, blue, and red colorway. articles.title: The Chicest Barefoot Sandal Yet Comes Courtesy of Literary Sport
articles.summary: Literary Sport, the Canadian label that has quietly become the new standard-bearer of chic activewear among fashion's inner circle, is now making a case for barefoot sandals.For its latest collaboration, the brand has teamed up with Xero Shoes to reinterpret the Genesis, the footwear company's original sandal first introduced in 2009 and inspired by the huarache-style sandals worn by Mexico's Tarahumara runners. The silhouette has remained largely unchanged for more than a decade, defined by its thin sole and minimalist lace construction.Rather than reinventing the formula, Literary Sport focuses on the sandal's most distinctive feature: its lacing system.
